text.title: security-architecture Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for security-architecture as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs hallucinate plausible-looking auth flows; OAuth/OIDC details (PKCE, audience, JWKS rotation) are frequently wrong. Pin to RFCs and provider docs.

failure-mode.description: Using LLM for cryptographic algorithm implementation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use established libraries instead. LLMs should not implement crypto algorithms.

failure-mode.description: Sharing sensitive data (credentials, keys) with LLM.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always use placeholders: [API_KEY], [SECRET]. Never paste actual secrets.

failure-mode.description: Skipping human review of security-critical changes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLM output is a starting point. Security experts should validate all security-critical changes.
